description: Analysis SOP & best practices for assay selection and environment-specific preprocessing.

sops.title: 16S amplicon vs shotgun metagenomics assay selection

decision.if: Need functional pathways (KEGG/COG/GO), AMR, strain/MAG, or unbiased discovery
Only community composition / cheap large cohort / well-characterized primers
limitations: Limited function; PICRUSt-like inference is hypothesis-generating only

decision.note: Escalate from 16S if pathways or biomarkers need functional support

checklist: State primary endpoint: taxonomy-only vs taxonomy+function
Budget / depth: 16S cheaper; shotgun needs deeper coverage for rare taxa/MAGs
Host DNA expected? Plan host depletion for host-associated shotgun
Metadata groups ready for differential analysis?

sops.title: Gut / host-associated sample preprocessing
steps: Raw QC: FastQC/MultiQC — track Q20/Q30, adapter content
Trim: fastp or Trimmomatic (Q20-oriented qualify / length filter)
Host removal: Bowtie2/KneadData against host genome index (paths.host_index)
Taxonomy: Kraken2 and/or MetaPhlAn; dual-path if configured
Function (shotgun): HUMAnN3 / DIAMOND→eggNOG for KEGG/COG
Stats: Alpha/Beta diversity + differential (metadata groups required)
pitfalls: High host DNA masquerades as low microbial diversity

sops.title: Soil sample preprocessing strategy
steps: Expect high eukaryotic / plant DNA — consider host/plant depletion if relevant
QC + aggressive adapter trim; soil libraries often have inhibitor-driven unevenness
Prefer MEGAHIT for complex assemblies; MetaBAT2 + CheckM2 for MAGs
Taxonomy: Kraken2 with broad DB; GTDB-Tk on MAGs
Function: DIAMOND/eggNOG; watch AMR/N-cycle pathways
Diversity: rarefaction / even sampling depth before Alpha/Beta

sops.title: Ocean / aquatic sample preprocessing strategy
steps: QC/trim; document filter size / collection metadata
Often low host but high viral fraction — consider ViWrap/PhaBOX when virome asked
Taxonomy with marine-aware or broad DBs; avoid gut-only interpretation
Assembly: MEGAHIT; binning + CheckM2; GTDB-Tk for prokaryotic MAGs
Function: focus carbon/nitrogen/sulfur pathway annotations
pitfalls: Viral DNA may dominate; prokaryote-only DBs under-classify

sops.title: Wastewater / WWTP sample preprocessing strategy
steps: QC/trim; document influent vs effluent / solids fraction metadata
Often mixed human + environmental DNA — optional human host filter if clinical ARG focus
Taxonomy: Kraken2 broad DB; expect high diversity and transient taxa
Assembly: MEGAHIT for complex sludge; MAG recovery recommended for large cohorts
Function + AMR: RGI/DeepARG/ResFinder — wastewater is ARG hotspot; report with caution
Stats: compositional methods (ANCOM-BC2 / MaAsLin3); control for plant/batch when multi-site
pitfalls: ARG abundance ≠ clinical risk without context
Strong batch effects across WWTPs and seasons

sops.title: Human fecal metagenomes — gene-centric functional annotation (Treiber 2020)
steps: Prefer gene-centric mapping (DIAMOND blastx / HUMAnN) over assemble-then-annotate when community function must not be biased to abundant taxa
Library: size-select to enable longer merged PE reads when possible
Reads: longer reads improve detection; merge PE when insert size allows
DIAMOND: use curated/custom protein DB when available; apply strict e-value (e.g. ≤1e-5) for high-confidence hits
Report sequencing depth sufficiency; avoid over-claiming rare pathways at low depth
Post: summarize KEGG/eggNOG/CAZy; bind claims to program tables
checklist: e-value / identity thresholds recorded in Methods
Host DNA removed for host-associated stool
citations: Treiber et al. 2020 BMC Biology — pre/post-sequencing recommendations

sops.title: Skin / early-life skin microbiome preprocessing
steps: Document body site, age, and sampling swab protocol (early-life catalogs are site-specific)
QC/trim; expect lower biomass — watch contamination and negative controls
Optional human host depletion; do not over-filter low-biomass microbial signal
Taxonomy with broad DB; MAG recovery when depth supports genome catalog goals
Interpret with skin genome catalogs (e.g. early-life skin MAG resources) rather than gut-only priors
pitfalls: Kit/reagent contaminants dominate low-biomass skin swabs
Gut marker taxa are not default priors

sops.title: Air / aerosol metagenomics (agricultural and environmental)
steps: Record filter type, flow rate, duration, weather, and crop/season metadata
Expect ultra-low biomass — mandatory negatives; DNA recovery and shotgun classification are feasible but noisy
Taxonomy to species when markers allow; track plant pathogens dynamically across season
Correlate pathogen spore proxies with weather covariates when metadata present
Avoid gut-centric interpretation of airborne communities

sops.title: Gut mycobiome / fungal shotgun analysis
steps: State endpoint: bacterial metagenome vs dedicated mycobiome (fungi are often <1% of shotgun reads)
Use eukaryotic-aware DBs / fungal genome catalogs (e.g. cultivated gut fungi resources) when available
Do not interpret absence of fungi as biological absence at shallow depth
For disease association, validate fungal signatures independently when possible
pitfalls: ITS amplicon may be preferred for dedicated mycobiome surveys

sops.title: Clinical respiratory mNGS — research orchestration SOP
steps: RESEARCH USE ONLY — not a certified IVD; document institutional ethics and validation status
Sample types: upper respiratory swab / BAL; track LoD, controls, and turnaround goals
Host depletion + viral-enriched or bulk metagenomics per study design
Taxonomy/virus detection with dual DNA/RNA awareness; retain discrepancy adjudication workflow
For novel virus discovery, enable de novo / translated-nucleotide style search when configured
Report sensitivity/specificity only when local validation exists
pitfalls: Do not claim clinical diagnosis from research agent alone

sops.title: Virus identification — multi-tool practice (benchmark-informed)
steps: Prefer ≥1 primary viral caller (VirSorter2 and/or geNomad) + CheckV quality
Biome matters: tool performance differs across gut/ocean/soil/air — disclose tools and cutoffs
Early-life gut virome: consider VLP-enriched vs bulk; age metadata improves interpretation
Do not treat single-tool hits as genome-quality viruses without CheckV / manual review
citations: Wu et al. 2024 virus ID benchmarking; Zeng et al. 2024 ELGV

sops.title: MAG QC gating (DAS Tool → CheckM2/BUSCO → GTDB-Tk / Bakta)
steps: Assemble (MEGAHIT/metaSPAdes/Flye) → bin (MetaBAT2/MaxBin2/CONCOCT/VAMB)
Refine with DAS Tool consensus across binners
CheckM2 + BUSCO: completeness & contamination / ortholog completeness
Gate: medium ≥50% comp / ≤10% contam; high ≥90% / ≤5%
GTDB-Tk classify_wf on passed bins; optional Bakta + ARG profiling on MAGs
citations: Banerjee et al. 2024 STAR Protocols MAG protocol

sops.title: Diversity and functional pathway reporting SOP
steps: Alpha: Shannon / Simpson / Observed richness per sample
Beta: Bray–Curtis + PCoA/NMDS; PERMANOVA if groups exist
Function: summarize KEGG/COG/GO pathway abundances (HUMAnN3/eggNOG)
Visualize: diversity plots, heatmap, volcano; write grounded biological narrative
pitfalls: Do not claim causality from cross-sectional diversity alone
